channel.title: Luki Belle | Dark Fantasy & Paranormal Fiction Author on Luki Belle – Fiction Author
channel.description: Recent content in Luki Belle | Dark Fantasy & Paranormal Fiction Author on Luki Belle – Fiction Author

item.title: Exploring Humanity in The Delicate Affaif Colonel Baquiste
item.description:
Exploring Humanity in The Delicate Affair of Colonel Baquiste

Delicate Affair is the phrase used by Ambassador General Masim Tropart when assigning the mission to Colonel Valstohl Baquiste—a mission that takes him into Southern Hastan under the guise of a naturalist, Gulaan Dasmire.

What unfolds is a story of profound transformation shaped not by force, but by subtle, persistent influence—set against a tense, unforgiving backdrop of colonization.

The “delicate affair” is not merely the mission itself, but the internal unraveling of Baquiste. Beneath his disguise, he carries the weight of physical suffering, a haunted past, and deeply rooted prejudice toward the people of Hastan. Yet, through quiet encounters and shifting perspectives, those convictions begin to fracture.

item.title: Scene 1 - Waiting Discharge at the Hospital in Hastan's Capital City
item.description:
A wounded Colonel Baquiste awaiting military discharge from the Monarchy

At the Hospital in the Capital City of Hastan

Colonel Valstohl Baquiste’s white cotton shirt was now sticking to his chest and torso from sweat. His rattan chair was in the shade of the verandah where the hot Hastan afternoon’s sun was gradually inching closer towards his right foot.

Yet it was the humidity of Hastan that was unbearable during the summer. The air was hard to breathe as though there was very little of it.

item.title: Scene 10 – Chamcham the little boy at the Yervaan Palace
item.description:
Chamcham the little boy at the Yervaan Palac

A boy entered from around the wooden room divider, dressed in a native Hastana garb with a pale pink sash around his waist and a colorful cloth wrapped elegantly over his small head. On his feet he had formal bamboo bark shoes. Upon his hands the boy was carrying a large copper tray laden with carafes of pink fruit juice and milk, a covered bowl, a covered plate, and a small vase with fresh flowers.

item.title: Scene 2 – Letter from Countess Rayaan Baquiste to her son Valstohl Baquiste
item.description:
Letter from Countess Rayaan Baquiste to her son, Colonel Valstohl Baquiste

“My son, if I were to console you on your physical loss then weakness will become your armor, if I were to tell you to face your loss like the brave man that you are, you would call me harsh. And so, I choose to say nothing about your present condition only to remind you that you are an army officer. It is a path you chose to follow like did your father, who did the same in the path of your grandfather. Though, I know you had little say in the matter.

My dear Valstohl, your last letter concerns me a great deal not because your sentiments are not valid but son you are a colonel now. And when you return you will do so with pride, not otherwise. Thus, it is with this regard for you that I say to you that you cannot return to me into my home son. I love you very much from the day you were born till today. Yet our lives were separated for a reason. I still am that woman who loves her freedom, more than her family. I wish I could have been the mother you wanted and I know you wish for that to change with time.

item.title: Scene 3 – Lieutenant Colonel Brune Farmagash
item.description:
Lieutenant Colonel Brune Farmagash delivers a message to Colonel Valstohl Baquiste

“You are not going home Colonel, sir. You have not been discharged from the army” Brune said while still looking outside, he started to slowly shake his head,“You have been chosen to finish something that has become more than a weak spark of rebellion. Presumably…you are the only one who can do this, Colonel sir” Brune said and then turned around to look at his superior.

“The Ambassador General wants to see you tomorrow sir, at 11 in the morning, at his residence. You will receive your orders there, Colonel Baquiste. I am to take you to the residence sir. You will be discharged from the hospital tomorrow, Colonel. Kindly pack your belongings tonight sir. I will arrive for you Colonel at 10:15 in the morning” Farmagash said.

item.title: Scene 4 – Ambassador General Masim Tropart
item.description:
Ambassador General Masim Tropart delivers the delicate mission to Colonel Baquiste

“It is a delicate affair Colonel Baquiste…because more than your ruthlessness and flair for killing the enemy, I need your cunning mind to penetrate a small colony of thugs! These rogues are causing some disturbances in a very specific area in the south and we need that land fully secured and tranquil!” Tropart said and put out his cigar. Then he leaned forward.

“You are fluent in Hastani – speaking and writing! You studied their traditions, history, and governance. But most importantly your present condition truly gave me the confidence that you are the man for this assignment! Not only would you fit the persona I needed, that of a high-ranking army officer to carry out this mission, but your disability would do the trick if you played your part right!” said Masim with a grin.

item.title: Scene 5 – Gulaan Dasmire - Valstohl Baquiste's Disguise
item.description:
The Disguise for Colonel Baquiste during the Delicate Mission...

"Gulaan Dasmire the Naturalist"

Masim smiled wickedly “And that is because of the mission! The Yervaan palace is surrounded by the marshy jungles! It is a short distance away from the Daikalin city. However, you will go there under a false identity and name…the new story of you is that of a naturalist…a disgruntled Llehstanzite…having left his motherland to study the enigmatic jungles of Daikalin region…and that, is just the beginning. You colonel can now adorn it however much you like” he said waving his fingers in the air as though he could not care about such details.

“You will appear within reach to the Yervaans and the society of Daikalin, a curious anthropologist in mind and in living philosophy! A very friendly Llehstanzite, not a colonizer! Someone who blends in with the natives…assimilate! You are yourself a rebel in exile…someone who wishes to immerse in their culture and welcomes the opportunity to prove that he is not like his colonial brothers! A peace-loving man who wants to find a middle ground. Become someone else…someone who is approachable and personable! That will make you interesting to the Southern Hastanas, and if you do it well and flaunt yourself timely… eventually to the thugs. But you must enter the jungles around the Yervaan palace and find a way to learn what lies in there and how these brutes manage to hide inside!” Tropart said in a sinister tone.

item.title: Scene 6 – Sergeant Solkian Frashtakh
item.description:
Sergeant Solkian Frashtakh

“It is Sergeant Solkian Frashtakh! Again! Greet me properly civilian!” shouted the officer.

Sergeant Frashtakh then rudely asked Gulaan for his identification documents and stiffened upon seeing that the civilian was carrying a dispatch stamp of authorization from Ambassador General Masim Tropart.

“So, the ambassador sends a disabled Llehstanzite civilian to help advance the education of our people!!” he smacked his thigh with his hand and shook his head as though he was thoroughly amused “Well better one like this one than a whole civilian!” he looked over at his men who agreed with his boorish humor and nodded.

item.title: Scene 7 – Bhaali the Yervaan Palace Caretaker
item.description:
Bhaali the caretaker of the Yervaan Palace

And then there was a creaking sound across the room from the direction of the wooden room divider, someone had pushed open a door. A warm glow appeared behind the wooden framed cloth divider and then the figure of a hunched over man, old as old can get, his face crunched, a big white moustache covering most of his small, wrinkled face. He entered carrying a lantern in one hand.

item.title: Scene 8– Madam Kaslini Yervaan
item.description:
Madam Kaslini Yervaan

She was perhaps in her early forties, lacking beauty. A thin rimmed eyeglass sat upon her nose bridge, her hair in a high bun with flower petals in it. Long, gold earrings draped her ears almost touching her shoulders. She was reading a book, Valstohl’s father’s notebook on ancient tribal martial arts, the one he had kept close to his body. Fragrance of night flowers exuded from her; it gave her an essence of freshness like a garden after the rains.

item.title: Scene 9 – Master Jeshn Yervaan
item.description:
Master Jeshn Yervaan

He was a man taller than Valstohl, with a well-trained athletic body, presently drenched in sweat, his brown skin showing through the sheer white top that clung to his upper body’s frame, accentuating his broad muscular chest and impressively big shoulders above strong biceps. Jeshn smiled broadly without showing his teeth, his finely defined square face was accented by an elegantly trimmed moustache with its ends curved upwards. His long proportioned and hooded eyes were in striking balance with the length and point of his nose, both exuding an eagle-like piercing gaze, which was now scanning Gulaan from head to toe and then back up.

item.title: The Delicate Affair of Colonel Baquiste
item.description:
A Mission Beyond Victory

After years of war, the Llehstanz Monarchy finally conquers the nation of Hastan under the command of Colonel Valstohl Baquiste, a brilliant but arrogant officer whose prejudice against the Hastanas is as strong as his reputation for victory. But when a final battle leaves him permanently wounded and his military career seemingly over, Baquiste receives one last assignment: a secret mission that will force him into unfamiliar territory where strategy alone may not be enough.

item.title: The Psychology of Colonel Baquiste
item.description:
The Psychology of Colonel Baquiste

Colonel Valstohl Baquiste is not merely a military figure—he is a study in contradiction.

Driven by discipline, shaped by war, and constrained by prejudice, his character reflects the internal conflict between authority and humanity.

Pride and Identity

Baquiste’s identity is rooted in conquest and hierarchy. His prejudice is not incidental—it is systemic, inherited from the structures he serves.

Transformation

What makes Baquiste compelling is not his strength, but his fracture. His journey forces him into unfamiliar terrain—both physically and psychologically.

item.title: What Lies Beneath the Front Cover of the Delicate Affair of Colonel Baquiste
item.description:
The Meaning Behind the Front Cover

The front cover of The Delicate Affair of Colonel Baquiste (TDACB) depicts a sunset scene upon the dangerous Ganis River, in Hastan, where on a rowboat you see the male protagonist, Colonel Valstohl Baquiste dressed in disguise of naturalist Gulaan Dasmire, looking at an elderly boatman who is focused on steering the boat because of the powerful river currents.

In the backdrop is the intense setting sun over densely populated forests. In the foreground, above the scene, are a pair of delicate silver anklets and below, a pair of vector-crossed swords with regal hilts, the blade of the left sword dripping with blood.

item.title: What will readers gain from reading The Delicate Affair of Colonel Baquiste
item.description:
Why Read The Delicate Affair of Colonel Baquiste

The Delicate Affair of Colonel Baquiste (TDACB) is a story from a fictitious colonization era set within two fictitious nations, Hastan and Llehstanz, nevertheless, the human elements showcased in this novel are very real and pertinent to our current times.

I wrote TDACB because of my personal life experiences having been born and grown up in post-colonial countries, having been on the receiving end of hate, racism, and prejudice.

item.title: Shakti of the Illuminated Lotus
item.description:
When the divine is exiled, power does not disappear—it transforms, waits, and rises again.

In a futuristic world shaped by ancient exile, hidden rulers and divine forces move in silence across continents. As a powerful king seeks a sacred weapon to reclaim what was lost, a ruthless rival rises to destroy it. Caught between them, a woman unknowingly becomes the key to a force that could reshape both the mortal and the divine.

item.title: The Vicious & The Virile VII
item.description:
When instinct awakens, humanity is no longer the dominant force.

Seven dark tales unravel where survival overrides morality and power takes monstrous forms. From twin destinies marked by violence to war between nature and mankind, each story reveals a world where instinct, brutality, and transformation shape fate beyond control.

Stories Included

  • Karan Lloyd Hamilton
  • Kartik Shiv Yuvarajan
  • Hounds of the Monks
  • Desert Frogs
  • Stolen Princess
  • Bastard of the Mist
  • Nature’s Call Girl

Synopsis

A collection of short horror stories, The Vicious and The Virile VII offers a captivating combination of unique and varied short stories emblematic of the dark fantasy genre.

item.title: Divine Weapons and Destiny
item.description:
Divine Weapons and Destiny

Power alone is not enough.

It must be focused.

Directed.

Chosen.

The Trident of Kumarun is not merely a weapon.

It is a threshold.

The Meaning of the Trident

It represents access.

To Juwala.

To origin.

To divine authority.

But not all who seek it wish to return.

Some wish to conquer.

Destiny and Worthiness

A divine weapon does not respond to strength alone.

It responds to alignment.

item.title: Featured Blog for The Sexy Seven Supernaturals - When Desire Summons the Supernatural
item.description:
When Desire Summons the Supernatural

There are forces we do not chase.

They arrive.

Quietly.

Uninvited—or so we believe.

In The Sexy Seven Supernaturals, desire is not passive. It is not a fleeting emotion or a passing curiosity. It is an active force—one that calls, shapes, and ultimately transforms reality.

Each protagonist does not simply encounter the supernatural.

She summons it.

The Nature of Invitation

Desire, especially when rooted in pain, loneliness, or longing, becomes something far more powerful than intention.

item.title: Scene I — The Awakening
item.description:
The Awakening

Power does not begin—it is remembered.

The ocean did not rage.

It shifted.

Beneath its surface, something ancient stirred— not summoned, not created— but released.

When she emerged, the world did not recognize her.

But it responded.

Storms bent.

Currents whispered.

And somewhere far beyond sight— something divine took notice.

Shakti was encircling the females.

Shakti the metamorphosed essence of Trishna, created by the force of the ocean.

item.title: Scene I — The Birth in Wilderness
item.description:
The Birth

Life begins in blood.

The jungle did not mourn.

It consumed.

As Priscilla screamed her last breath into the wilderness, the earth answered—not with mercy, but with continuation.

Two lives emerged from death.

Only one was taken.

The other— was claimed by something older than man.

The tribal people had pulled her out of the river, the tigress that had attacked Priscilla in the boat had saved her from drowning by clenching on to her belly. Four strong tribal men had swum into the water and had rescued the bleeding pregnant woman and the beast. Until then, both woman and tigress were drifting fast through the river where the currents had picked up beyond the downstream fork.

item.title: Scene I — The Photo and The Letter
item.description:
The Invitation

Desire does not knock. It waits to be noticed.

It was a glass picture frame, a photo of Sasha sitting in a stunning wedding dress in a fully blossomed garden, against the backdrop of a magnificent mansion. And though I had seen many pictures of her during that evening, in exotic locations, this one stood out because she looked quite happy. There were flowers in her hair and the sun’s perfect angle upon her face was making her glow.

item.title: Scene II — The Exile
item.description:
The Exile

Divinity does not forgive—it transforms.

Kumarun did not raise his voice.

He did not need to.

The silence itself carried judgment.

Kunjahl felt it first— the unraveling of belonging.

Ahankara smiled.

Because exile, to him, was not punishment.

It was opportunity.

Ahankara was right in his foretelling that Trishna’s effect upon the humans would magnify yet he was not one to keep her captive.

Within her aura was both power and weakness and only the wise and cunning could know how to stay strong without falling prey to Trishna’s charm.

item.title: Scene II — The Predator in Manhattan
item.description:
The Predator

Civilization hides nothing.

Manhattan glittered.

Alive.

Unaware.

Karan moved through it like a shadow with intention.

The scar on his body pulsed—

not as a wound.

But as a signal.

Night did not change him.

It revealed him.

← Previous Next →

item.title: Scene II — The Strength of Feathers
item.description:
Dare to Let Go

Fear is only the body remembering its limits.

Vanessa looked to her left and saw that where the triangular observation deck angled off, a woman and a man were standing there.

It was Lady Eliza and Sebastian Drake. He was looking out into the skyline.

Lady Eliza was standing close to him while looking towards Vanessa, she had a smile on her face.

item.title: Scene III — The Aunt
item.description:
Seek and Become Sought After

Love becomes dangerous when it pursues.

“Ah child! You are gifted…so gifted…you tempt me…ah you tempt me so!” Zaylinar’s hoarse voice reverberated in the room as she restrained her claws.

At the tender age of fifteen Anika’s world was filled with studies which did not hold her interest, dancing which was her passion and watching her uncle practice fighting.

Anika was not allowed to train for the army, neither were any of her close girlfriends who had all been married off.

item.title: Scene III — The Hunt to the Monastery
item.description:
The Hunt

No place is sacred.

The monastery stood in silence.

But silence is not safety.

They came not as soldiers—

but as something worse.

Men with hunger beyond command.

Beyond reason.

And inside—

something else was waiting.

The doors of the monastery noisily opened as though the hinges had not been used in the longest time and lacked grease.

Shamini slowly stood up and called to her daughter, but Zehera had fainted.

item.title: Scene III — The Panther
item.description:
The Panther

Guardians do not choose—they are chosen.

It moved without sound.

Without hesitation.

The panther did not belong to the Western world.

Yet it walked through it as though it had always been there.

Its eyes carried something older than memory.

And something far more dangerous than instinct.

It was not hunting.

It was searching.

“I am AlexanDRA, king of the Western Continent and I welcome you, to my kingdom!” he spoke in the ancient Eastern Continent’s dialect, in a deep voice and slowly moved from where he was towards her, staying low in a crouching position, his eyes fixed upon hers.

item.title: Scene IV — Ravenous
item.description:
Soul-Wrenching Hunger

Kindness can be a disguise for an unsatiated appetite.

His pitch-dark pupils looked down upon the girl, at her long black hair resting upon her slender waist.

Her green eyes looked up through the rays of the sun coming through the trees. Her lips pressed in from anger at the sight of the ravens. He saw the delicate sweat beads around the mounds of her breasts where the fair flesh was exposed below the unbuttoned blouse.

item.title: Scene IV — The Military View on The Desert Tribe
item.description:
The Desert

War begins before the first shot.

The sand remembered everything.

Blood.

Secrets.

Deals made in silence.

The film crew thought they were observers.

They were already part of the story.

And the desert—

was choosing sides.

“Truth changes with time and times change people. There are those under Daezar who believe they must assimilate and change, for the continuation of the nomadic tradition. And there are those in the military, of nomadic origin, who secretly believe that their tradition must be kept untouched”

item.title: Scene IV — The Ocean
item.description:
The Ocean

When balance breaks, nature answers.

The waves did not crash.

They rose.

Cities watched as the horizon distorted— as though reality itself had begun to bend.

Something had been disturbed.

Something ancient.

And it would not remain silent.

The ocean was no longer a boundary.

It was becoming a force.

“They are striking and burning her don’t you think?” Sol asked maliciously.

“You mean giving her pleasure” replied the professor.

item.title: Scene V — The Bride's Matchmaker
item.description:
Wide-Eyed Infatuations

Bride beware.

Mylakena almost recoiled from the man from the touch of his hands upon her face.

He then said to her “I am Krajick, Jerallenar’s father. Welcome my soon to be daughter-in-law Mylakena” he bent his head forward and kissed her forehead while letting his hands slide down to her shoulders.

A ripple of fear ran down Mylakena’s spine “Where is my uncle?” Mylakena asked nervously.

item.title: Scene V — The Mist Encounters The Gifted Woman
item.description:
The Mist

Not all healing is salvation.

The forest watched.

Always.

Angeline felt it the moment she arrived.

Not danger—

but recognition.

Something in the mist knew her.

And it was not afraid.

As she was about to continue walking when the mist suddenly emerged all around her and quickly became dense. It completely blinded her vision. Angeline could hear only her own panicked breathing.

“Mom! Can you see anything? Mom!” the owl hooted. “Oh no! This is not supposed to happen! The pamphlet said the mist only comes out at night!” she said nervously.

item.title: Scene V — The Release
item.description:
The Release

Some forces are not meant to be controlled.

Ahankara did not hesitate.

The void opened— not as a prison, but as a doorway.

A pair of golden eyes upon a milky white face looked down at her.

The girl child stopped crying and smiled. Strands of golden hair fell gently upon the child’s face, and she touched them with her tiny hands.

Viratgarviathan emerged slowly, its presence bending the world around it.

item.title: Scene VI — The Convergence
item.description:
The Convergence

All forces move toward the same point.

The Trident had not been found.

It had been waiting.

For centuries.

For intention.

For alignment.

He took her hand and kissed the top of her palm “Find me the Panther King Suraj and his clan. They guard the Trident of Kumarun. The only thing we need to enter Juwala!” he commanded, squeezing her palm in his hand.

Now, everything moved toward it.

Kings.

item.title: Scene VI — The Old Willow Tree
item.description:
Nature is Always Watching

Never underestimate a weeping willow.

“Hello! I am Helen and I was really attracted to your home. I was jogging in the neighborhood and did not realize the brick lane would lead to this property. Are you the owner of this gorgeous place?” she stood a few inches away from him on the steps of the porch.

The man shook his head.

item.title: Scene VI — The War Begins
item.description:
The War

Nature does not negotiate.

Parakrytheon believed it had won.

No forests.

No beasts.

No threats.

But nature does not disappear.

It adapts.

Waits.

Strikes.

And when it does—

there are no survivors.

Only witnesses.

From the broken glass dome of Parakrytheon a metallic object shot out and instantly opened up into a massive parachute which then collapsed back down upon the dome, sealing Parakrytheon from the wild savages outside. The darkness of the evening had settled all around.

item.title: Test Book
item.description:
Test Book

If you can see this, routing works.

item.title: The Feminine and the Unknown
item.description:
The Feminine and the Unknown

There is a quiet power in the unknown.

A presence that does not demand attention— but reshapes everything it touches.

The women in The Sexy Seven Supernaturals do not seek power in the traditional sense.

They seek understanding.

Escape.

Connection.

And in doing so, they step into something far older than themselves.

Beyond Control

The feminine in these stories is not defined by control.

It is defined by openness.

item.title: The Origin of the Asukhas
item.description:
The Origin of the Asukhas

Before power was divided, it was unified.

Before exile, there was belonging.

The Asukhas were not always rulers of hidden worlds.

They were once disciples.

Students of divine knowledge within the sacred realm of Juwala.

The First Fracture

The banishment of Kunjahl and Ahankara was not merely punishment.

It was a fracture in the structure of divinity itself.

What was once contained within the divine realm was released into the mortal world.

item.title: The Power of Exile
item.description:
The Power of Exile

Exile is often seen as loss.

But in truth—

it is transformation.

To be removed from origin is to be forced into becoming.

Beyond Belonging

Kunjahl and Ahankara did not lose power when they were cast out.

They lost limitation.

Freed from divine structure, they entered a world where power could evolve unchecked.

The Dual Nature of Exile

Exile creates two paths:

One seeks return.

The other seeks domination.

item.title: The Psychology of Invitation
item.description:
The Psychology of Invitation

Not all invitations are conscious.

Some are built slowly— through memory, fear, longing, and silence.

In these stories, the supernatural is not random.

It is precise.

It responds to something deeply embedded within each character.

The Inner Signal

Every protagonist carries an internal signal.

A frequency shaped by past experiences, emotional wounds, and unspoken desires.

The supernatural recognizes this signal.

And answers it.

The Illusion of Control

We believe we control what enters our lives.

item.title: The Rise of Shakti
item.description:
The Rise of Shakti

Shakti is not created.

She is awakened.

When imbalance reaches its peak—

something responds.

The Emergence

The ocean did not give birth by chance.

It responded to disruption.

To imbalance.

To violence.

And from that response—

something new emerged.

Beyond Power

Shakti is not simply strength.

She is balance.

Creation.

Destruction.

Transformation.

She is what restores what has been broken—

and what redefines what cannot be restored.

item.title: The Sexy Seven Supernaturals
item.description:
Some desires don’t fade…they summon something.

What answers when longing calls…is never what you expect.

Seven haunting tales of women and girls whose lives are forever altered by mysterious supernatural encounters.

Each story explores the fragile boundary between emotional longing and unseen forces—where grief, ambition, fear, and desire open doors that cannot be closed.

From a heartbroken woman drawn into a stranger’s past, to a young girl caught between love and war, to a student pulled into the presence of something ancient and watchful—each narrative reveals a chilling truth:

item.title: When Longing Becomes Dangerous
item.description:
When Longing Becomes Dangerous

Longing is not harmless.

It is not passive.

It is a force that moves toward fulfillment.

And sometimes—

it chooses paths we would never consciously take.

The Threshold

There is always a moment.

A quiet threshold.

Where desire shifts from thought…

to action.

In that moment, something irreversible begins.

The Price of Fulfillment

To have what one deeply desires is not always liberation.

Sometimes, it is transformation.
